name: omarchy_monitors
content:
Monitors 
Omarchy assumes you're running on a 2x-capabe retina-cass dispay by defaut. This is
what you need to get those nice, crisp programmer fonts. It's what aptops ike the
Framework 13 with it's 2.8K monitor is optimized for. It's what you'd want to run on a 27"
5K Appe Studio Dispay/ProArt PA27JCV/Samsung S9/Kuycon G27P or 32" 6K Appe XDR/
ProArt PA32QCV/Kuycon G32P.
So if you're not running a dispay with a PPI of 218 or above, you' want to change the
monitor settings. For exampe, if you have a 27" or 32" 4K, you can use fractiona scaing
by opening ~/.config/hypr/monitors.conf and switching to the recommendation for that
combo:
env = GDK_SCALE,1.75
monitor=,preferred,auto,1.666667
If you're using a 1080p or 1440p dispay, you' probaby just want to use 1x scaing, so you
can use:
env = GDK_SCALE,1
monitor=,preferred,auto,1
Changes to GDK_SCALE  appy to appications started after the change. So make sure you
quit the windows that you have that are oversized after the change (or cose a windows
with Ctrl + Alt + Del!.
Just know that it's running Linux on ow-resoution dispays and using fractiona scaing
that's given the patform a bad reputation for fussy fonts. It's an entirey sef-inficted
situation. With a retina-cass dispay 218ppi+) and 2x scaing, your fonts wi ook every
bit as great on Linux as they woud on the Mac (if not better, because no artificia font
smoothening above what the font designer intended is appied!.
Arranging mutipe screens 
Hyprand works great with mutipe screens. Read more about how to ay them out in the
Hyprand monitor documentation. You can bind specific workspaces to specific monitors
as we.
You can aso checkout Hyprmon, if you'd ike a TUI to hep you with the positioning of
mutipe screens.
Monitors · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/86/monitors
